message:
feat(websocket): add handshake + close protocol, refactor to module dir

Restructure WebSocket from single file to directory module:
- frame.rs: RFC 6455 wire format (moved from websocket.rs)
- handshake.rs: HTTP upgrade negotiation (bd-1kg0)
- close.rs: Close handshake state machine (bd-1bmj)

Handshake implementation:
- WsUrl parsing for ws:// and wss:// URLs
- ClientHandshake for generating upgrade requests
- ServerHandshake for accepting upgrades
- Sec-WebSocket-Accept validation per RFC 6455

Dependencies:
- Add sha1 crate for Sec-WebSocket-Accept calculation
- Make base64 unconditional (was tls-only)

All 46 WebSocket tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jkx","title":"Debug console E2E test suite: TUI, diagnostics, and visualization tests","description":"# Debug Console E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive end-to-end test suite for the runtime debug console covering
TUI interactions, diagnostic queries, and visualization rendering.

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/console/
├── mod.rs                       # Test module root
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs                   # Shared utilities
│   ├── runtime_fixtures.rs      # Pre-built runtime states
│   └── snapshot.rs              # Snapshot testing utilities
├── rendering/
│   ├── terminal_detect.rs       # Terminal capability tests
│   ├── color_modes.rs           # Color rendering tests
│   ├── styled_text.rs           # Style application tests
│   └── unicode.rs               # Unicode width tests
├── region_tree/
│   ├── simple_tree.rs           # Basic tree rendering
│   ├── nested_tree.rs           # Deep hierarchy tests
│   ├── status_icons.rs          # Status indicator tests
│   ├── budget_bars.rs           # Budget visualization tests
│   └── live_updates.rs          # Dynamic update tests
├── diagnostics/
│   ├── explain_region.rs        # Region open explanation
│   ├── explain_task.rs          # Task blocked explanation
│   ├── explain_cancel.rs        # Cancellation trace tests
│   ├── find_leaks.rs            # Obligation leak detection
│   └── recommendations.rs       # Actionable fix tests
├── tui/
│   ├── navigation.rs            # Keyboard navigation
│   ├── expand_collapse.rs       # Tree expand/collapse
│   ├── filtering.rs             # Status filtering
│   └── search.rs                # Search functionality
└── lab/
    ├── deterministic.rs         # Lab runtime tests
    └── scripted_scenarios.rs    # Scripted state progressions
```

## Test Categories

### 1. Rendering Tests
- Terminal capability detection
- Color support detection and downgrade
- Styled text output
- Unicode width calculation
- ANSI escape sequences

### 2. Region Tree Tests
- Simple tree rendering
- Deeply nested hierarchies
- Status icons (running, draining, etc.)
- Budget bar visualization
- Live update rendering

### 3. Diagnostic Tests
- explain_region_open accuracy
- explain_task_blocked accuracy
- Cancellation path tracing
- Obligation leak detection
- Recommendation generation

### 4. TUI Interaction Tests
- Keyboard navigation
- Region expand/collapse
- Status filtering
- Search by name/ID

### 5. Snapshot Tests
- Golden file comparisons
- Regression detection
- Output consistency

## Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual render calls
- DEBUG: Query execution, tree traversal
- INFO: Test progress, diagnostics found
- WARN: Rendering issues
- ERROR: Test failures with output diffs

## Test Scripts

### Run All Console Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync=debug,test=info"
export RUST_BACKTRACE=1

echo "=== Debug Console E2E Tests ==="
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_console -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee console_tests.log

echo "=== Results ==="
grep -E '(PASS|FAIL|test result)' console_tests.log
```

### Update Snapshots
```bash
# When rendering changes intentionally, update golden files
BLESS=1 cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_console snapshots:: -- --nocapture
```

### TUI Interactive Test
```bash
# Manual TUI testing (requires terminal)
cargo run --example console_tui_demo
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All rendering tests pass
- [ ] Region tree renders correctly
- [ ] Diagnostics provide accurate info
- [ ] TUI interactions work
- [ ] Snapshot tests match golden files
- [ ] Lab runtime tests deterministic
- [ ] Structured logging
- [ ] CI integration (non-interactive tests)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:52:55.246592149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:30.713276958Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","ecosystem-parity","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jkx","depends_on_id":"bd-1ctm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:53:13.486005313Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jkx","depends_on_id":"bd-23lw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:30.713244377Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jkx","depends_on_id":"bd-257b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:28.499392852Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jkx","depends_on_id":"bd-nugw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:53:11.120013187Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jkx","depends_on_id":"bd-qctz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:53:15.829738866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jkx","depends_on_id":"bd-vpkn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:53:08.704723982Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jkx","depends_on_id":"bd-zd26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:26.277172626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jp7","title":"Blocking pool: regression tests for shutdown and thread management","description":"# Task: Blocking Pool Regression Tests

## Overview

After fixing the blocking pool bugs (shutdown_and_wait, thread handle joining,
spawn from handle), we need comprehensive regression tests to ensure:
1. Thread joins work correctly on shutdown
2. Active thread count is accurate
3. Spawning from BlockingPoolHandle works
4. No thread leaks under stress
5. Queue management is correct
6. Thread parking/waking works properly

## Bug Fixes Being Tested

### Fix 1: shutdown_and_wait thread joining
- Previously: Tried to join before shutdown, handles not accessible
- Fixed: Wait for active_threads counter to reach 0, then join handles
- Test: Verify all threads terminate within timeout

### Fix 2: Thread handles moved to BlockingPoolInner
- Previously: Handles in BlockingPool, not accessible from BlockingPoolHandle
- Fixed: thread_handles in BlockingPoolInner behind Arc
- Test: Verify spawning from both Pool and Handle works

### Fix 3: Active thread counter accuracy
- Previously: Counter could drift if spawn failed
- Fixed: Increment on spawn success, decrement on thread exit
- Test: Stress spawn/exit and verify counter matches reality

## Unit Tests

### Pool Creation Tests
- [ ] `test_pool_new_min_max` - Create pool with min/max threads
- [ ] `test_pool_new_zero_min` - Zero min threads allowed
- [ ] `test_pool_new_min_equals_max` - Fixed-size pool
- [ ] `test_pool_new_invalid_config` - max < min rejected
- [ ] `test_pool_default` - Default configuration works

### Shutdown Tests
- [ ] `test_shutdown_and_wait_empty_pool` - No tasks, immediate shutdown
- [ ] `test_shutdown_and_wait_pending_tasks` - Tasks complete then shutdown
- [ ] `test_shutdown_and_wait_timeout_respected` - Timeout triggers correctly
- [ ] `test_shutdown_and_wait_returns_false_on_timeout` - Return value correct
- [ ] `test_shutdown_all_threads_joined` - No zombie threads (verify via ps)
- [ ] `test_shutdown_idempotent` - Multiple shutdown calls harmless
- [ ] `test_shutdown_then_spawn` - Spawn after shutdown rejected
- [ ] `test_shutdown_wakes_parked_workers` - All workers wake on shutdown
- [ ] `test_shutdown_tasks_complete` - In-flight tasks finish

### Thread Spawning Tests
- [ ] `test_spawn_from_pool` - Spawn via BlockingPool::spawn
- [ ] `test_spawn_from_handle` - Spawn via BlockingPoolHandle::spawn
- [ ] `test_spawn_handle_clone` - Cloned handle spawns correctly
- [ ] `test_spawn_scales_up` - Threads grow when needed
- [ ] `test_spawn_scales_to_max` - Never exceeds max_threads
- [ ] `test_spawn_respects_min` - At least min_threads always running
- [ ] `test_spawn_under_load` - High-frequency spawning works
- [ ] `test_spawn_return_value` - spawn() returns handle or error

### Counter Tests
- [ ] `test_active_threads_starts_at_min` - Initial count equals min_threads
- [ ] `test_active_threads_accuracy` - Counter matches actual thread count
- [ ] `test_active_threads_on_panic` - Counter decrements on task panic
- [ ] `test_active_threads_on_spawn_fail` - Counter not incremented on failure
- [ ] `test_active_threads_shrinks` - Counter decreases when threads exit
- [ ] `test_active_threads_concurrent_updates` - No races in counter

### Queue Management Tests
- [ ] `test_queue_fifo` - Tasks execute in order (when single worker)
- [ ] `test_queue_length` - Queue length accurate
- [ ] `test_queue_unbounded` - Queue accepts many tasks
- [ ] `test_queue_empty_on_shutdown` - Queue drains before shutdown
- [ ] `test_queue_concurrent_push` - Multiple spawners safe

### Thread Parking Tests
- [ ] `test_worker_parks_on_empty` - Idle workers park
- [ ] `test_worker_wakes_on_task` - Parked worker wakes for new task
- [ ] `test_worker_idle_timeout` - Excess workers exit after idle time
- [ ] `test_worker_min_never_exits` - Min threads don't exit on idle
- [ ] `test_worker_park_notify_race` - No lost wakeups

### Task Execution Tests
- [ ] `test_task_result_returned` - spawn_blocking returns result
- [ ] `test_task_panic_caught` - Panic doesn't crash pool
- [ ] `test_task_panic_propagated` - Panic can be retrieved from handle
- [ ] `test_task_cancellation` - Cancelled tasks don't execute
- [ ] `test_task_priority` - Priority ordering if supported

### Thread Naming Tests
- [ ] `test_thread_names` - Workers have meaningful names
- [ ] `test_thread_name_prefix` - Custom prefix supported
- [ ] `test_thread_name_unique` - Each worker uniquely named

### Handle Lifecycle Tests
- [ ] `test_handle_outlives_pool` - Handle valid after pool dropped
- [ ] `test_handle_clone_independence` - Clones are independent
- [ ] `test_handle_spawn_after_pool_drop` - Behavior defined

## Stress Tests

```rust
#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_blocking_pool_churn() {
    // 10,000 tasks with varied durations
    // Verify no thread leaks, all complete
    let pool = BlockingPool::new(4, 16);
    let completed = AtomicU64::new(0);
    let rng = rand::thread_rng();
    
    for i in 0..10_000 {
        let c = completed.clone();
        let sleep_us = (i % 1000) * 10; // 0-10ms
        pool.spawn(move || {
            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_micros(sleep_us));
            c.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        });
    }
    
    // Should complete well within 30s
    assert!(pool.shutdown_and_wait(Duration::from_secs(30)),
        "Pool did not shutdown in time");
    assert_eq!(completed.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 10_000,
        "Not all tasks completed");
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_blocking_pool_shutdown_race() {
    // Spawn tasks while shutting down
    // Verify: no panics, no leaks, deterministic behavior
    for iteration in 0..100 {
        let pool = BlockingPool::new(2, 8);
        let handle = pool.handle();
        let spawned = Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0));
        
        let s = spawned.clone();
        let spawner = thread::spawn(move || {
            for _ in 0..100 {
                if handle.spawn(|| {}).is_ok() {
                    s.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
                }
            }
        });
        
        // Race: shutdown while spawning
        thread::sleep(Duration::from_micros(100));
        pool.shutdown();
        
        spawner.join().expect("Spawner panicked");
        
        assert!(pool.shutdown_and_wait(Duration::from_secs(5)),
            "Iteration {} failed to shutdown", iteration);
    }
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_blocking_pool_counter_accuracy() {
    // Rapid spawn/complete cycles
    // Periodically verify counter matches reality
    let pool = BlockingPool::new(2, 32);
    let barrier = Arc::new(std::sync::Barrier::new(101));
    
    for _ in 0..100 {
        let b = barrier.clone();
        pool.spawn(move || {
            b.wait();
        });
    }
    
    // All 100 tasks waiting at barrier, counter should reflect this
    thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
    let active = pool.active_threads();
    assert!(active >= 2 && active <= 32,
        "Active threads {} out of bounds", active);
    
    barrier.wait(); // Release all tasks
    
    pool.shutdown_and_wait(Duration::from_secs(10));
}

#[test]
#[ignore]
fn stress_test_blocking_pool_panic_recovery() {
    // Many tasks that panic
    // Verify pool continues functioning
    let pool = BlockingPool::new(4, 8);
    let completed = AtomicU64::new(0);
    
    for i in 0..1000 {
        let c = completed.clone();
        pool.spawn(move || {
            if i % 10 == 0 {
                panic!("Intentional panic {}", i);
            }
            c.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        });
    }
    
    assert!(pool.shutdown_and_wait(Duration::from_secs(30)));
    // 900 tasks should complete (100 panicked)
    assert_eq!(completed.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 900);
}
```

## E2E Test Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/test_blocking_pool_e2e.sh

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
LOG_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/test_logs/blocking_pool_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

echo "=== Blocking Pool E2E Test Suite ==="
echo "Log directory: $LOG_DIR"
echo "Start time: $(date -Iseconds)"

# Unit tests
echo ""
echo "[1/4] Running unit tests..."
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --lib runtime::blocking_pool -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/unit_tests.log"
UNIT_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}

# Stress tests
echo ""
echo "[2/4] Running stress tests (timeout: 120s)..."
timeout 120s cargo test --lib --release blocking_pool_stress -- --ignored --nocapture --test-threads=1 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/stress_tests.log"
STRESS_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}

# Thread leak check with helgrind
echo ""
echo "[3/4] Checking for thread issues..."
if command -v valgrind &>/dev/null; then
    cargo test --lib blocking_pool_basic --release --no-run 2>/dev/null
    TEST_BIN=$(find target/release/deps -name "asupersync-*" -type f -executable | head -1)
    if [ -n "$TEST_BIN" ]; then
        timeout 60s valgrind --tool=helgrind --error-exitcode=1 "$TEST_BIN" --test blocking_pool 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/helgrind.log" || true
        HELGRIND_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    else
        echo "Test binary not found"
        HELGRIND_EXIT=0
    fi
else
    echo "Valgrind not available, skipping"
    HELGRIND_EXIT=0
fi

# Generate summary
echo ""
echo "[4/4] Generating summary..."

cat > "$LOG_DIR/summary.md" << EOF
# Blocking Pool Test Report

## Date: $(date -Iseconds)

## Test Results

| Suite | Status |
|-------|--------|
| Unit Tests | $([ $UNIT_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL") |
| Stress Tests | $([ $STRESS_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "❌ FAIL") |
| Helgrind | $([ $HELGRIND_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo "✅ PASS" || echo "⚠️ ISSUES") |

## Test Counts
$(grep -hE "^test result:" "$LOG_DIR"/*.log 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A")

## Failures
$(grep -hE "(FAILED|panicked)" "$LOG_DIR"/*.log 2>/dev/null | head -10 || echo "None")

## Thread Issues
$(grep -hE "(race|deadlock|leak)" "$LOG_DIR/helgrind.log" 2>/dev/null | head -10 || echo "None detected")
EOF

cat "$LOG_DIR/summary.md"

echo ""
echo "End time: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "Logs saved to: $LOG_DIR"
echo "=== Test Complete ==="

# Exit with failure if unit or stress tests failed
[ $UNIT_EXIT -eq 0 ] && [ $STRESS_EXIT -eq 0 ]
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
tracing::info!(
    pool = "blocking",
    min_threads = pool.min_threads,
    max_threads = pool.max_threads,
    active = pool.active_threads.load(Ordering::Acquire),
    queued = pool.queue.len(),
    "Blocking pool state"
);

tracing::debug!(
    pool = "blocking",
    thread_id = ?std::thread::current().id(),
    thread_name = ?std::thread::current().name(),
    "Worker spawned"
);

tracing::debug!(
    pool = "blocking",
    thread_id = ?std::thread::current().id(),
    tasks_executed = task_count,
    elapsed_ms = elapsed.as_millis(),
    reason = "idle_timeout",
    "Worker exiting"
);

tracing::trace!(
    pool = "blocking",
    thread_id = ?std::thread::current().id(),
    task_id = task.id,
    "Task starting"
);

tracing::warn!(
    pool = "blocking",
    thread_id = ?std::thread::current().id(),
    task_id = task.id,
    panic_msg = ?panic_info,
    "Task panicked"
);
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 40+ unit tests implemented and passing
- [ ] Stress tests run 60s without thread leaks
- [ ] active_threads counter always accurate (verified with assertions)
- [ ] Shutdown always completes within timeout
- [ ] Panic recovery works correctly
- [ ] No helgrind warnings (data races, deadlocks)
- [ ] E2E script runs in CI with proper exit codes
- [ ] Structured logging for debugging
- [ ] Thread names visible in debugger/top","notes":"Implementation complete: 36+ regression tests added.  {"id":"bd-1js3","title":"ActorContext: Cx extension with actor capabilities","description":"# ActorContext: Actor-Specific Capabilities

## Goal

Extend Cx with actor-specific capabilities, providing the interface actors use for all runtime interactions.

## Background

Actors need everything regular tasks have (cancellation, budgets, I/O) plus actor-specific operations:
- Self-reference (ActorRef to self)
- Child spawning (supervised children)
- Stopping (self and children)
- Parent reference (for escalation)

## ActorContext Design

```rust
pub struct ActorContext<'a> {
    /// Underlying capability context
    cx: &'a mut Cx,
    
    /// Reference to this actor
    self_ref: ActorRef<Self::Message>,
    
    /// This actor's ID
    actor_id: ActorId,
    
    /// Parent supervisor (None for root actors)
    parent: Option<ActorRef<SupervisorMessage>>,
    
    /// Children currently supervised by this actor
    children: Vec<ActorId>,
}
```

## Capabilities

### Self-Reference
```rust
// Get a reference to self (for giving to other actors)
fn self_ref(&self) -> ActorRef<Self::Message>;

// Get own actor ID
fn actor_id(&self) -> ActorId;
```

### Child Management
```rust
// Spawn a supervised child actor
async fn spawn_child<A: Actor>(
    &mut self,
    actor: A,
    mailbox_config: MailboxConfig,
    supervision_config: SupervisionConfig,
) -> ActorRef<A::Message>;

// Stop a child actor
async fn stop_child(&mut self, child: ActorId);

// Stop all children
async fn stop_all_children(&mut self);

// List current children
fn children(&self) -> &[ActorId];
```

### Self-Termination
```rust
// Request own termination (graceful)
fn stop_self(&mut self);

// Check if stop was requested
fn is_stopping(&self) -> bool;
```

### Parent Interaction
```rust
// Get parent supervisor reference
fn parent(&self) -> Option<ActorRef<SupervisorMessage>>;

// Escalate error to parent
async fn escalate(&mut self, error: Error);
```

### Delegation to Cx
```rust
// All Cx methods available via Deref or delegation
fn checkpoint(&self) -> Result<(), Cancelled>;
fn is_cancel_requested(&self) -> bool;
fn budget(&self) -> Budget;
fn now(&self) -> Time;
fn trace(&self, event: TraceEvent);
// ... etc
```

## Implementation Notes

- ActorContext holds &mut Cx internally
- Deref<Target=Cx> for transparent access to Cx methods
- Child list kept in sync with RuntimeState
- Stopping flag separate from cancellation (can stop without cancel)

## Lifecycle Integration

ActorContext methods interact with lifecycle:
- spawn_child() only valid in Running state
- stop_self() transitions to Stopping state
- is_stopping() reflects lifecycle, not just cancellation

## Testing

- Self-reference usable for tell() patterns
- Child spawning creates supervised relationship
- stop_self() triggers graceful shutdown
- Cx methods accessible through ActorContext

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ActorContext struct with all fields
- [ ] Self-reference methods
- [ ] Child management methods
- [ ] Self-termination methods
- [ ] Parent interaction methods
- [ ] Transparent Cx delegation
- [ ] Integration with actor lifecycle states","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:19:56.050864288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:50.702274444Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["actors","context","phase3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1js3","depends_on_id":"bd-3dcp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:50.702211998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1js3","depends_on_id":"bd-3t9g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:19:56.073383378Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1js3","depends_on_id":"bd-trgk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:48.602178888Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1kg0","title":"WebSocket handshake: HTTP upgrade negotiation","description":"# WebSocket Handshake Implementation

## Goal

Implement the WebSocket opening handshake (HTTP upgrade) for both client and server roles.

## Background

WebSocket connections start as HTTP/1.1 and upgrade via the Upgrade header mechanism.

### Client Request
```http
GET /chat HTTP/1.1
Host: server.example.com
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Key: dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBub25jZQ==
Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13
Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: chat, superchat
Sec-WebSocket-Extensions: permessage-deflate
```

### Server Response
```http
HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Accept: s3pPLMBiTxaQ9kYGzzhZRbK+xOo=
Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: chat
```

## Types to Implement

### ClientHandshake
```rust
pub struct ClientHandshake {
    /// WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://)
    url: Url,
    
    /// Random 16-byte key, base64 encoded
    key: String,
    
    /// Requested subprotocols (optional)
    protocols: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Requested extensions (optional)
    extensions: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Additional headers
    headers: HeaderMap,
}

impl ClientHandshake {
    /// Generate the HTTP upgrade request
    pub fn request(&self) -> Request<()>;
    
    /// Validate server response
    pub fn validate_response(&self, response: &Response<()>) -> Result<(), HandshakeError>;
}
```

### ServerHandshake
```rust
pub struct ServerHandshake {
    /// Supported subprotocols
    supported_protocols: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Supported extensions
    supported_extensions: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Custom validation callback
    validator: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&Request<()>) -> bool>>,
}

impl ServerHandshake {
    /// Validate client request and generate response
    pub fn accept(&self, request: &Request<()>) -> Result<Response<()>, HandshakeError>;
    
    /// Reject with custom status code
    pub fn reject(status: StatusCode) -> Response<()>;
}
```

### HandshakeError
```rust
pub enum HandshakeError {
    /// Invalid HTTP request
    InvalidRequest(String),
    
    /// Missing required header
    MissingHeader(&'static str),
    
    /// Invalid Sec-WebSocket-Key
    InvalidKey,
    
    /// Invalid Sec-WebSocket-Accept (response validation)
    InvalidAccept,
    
    /// Unsupported WebSocket version
    UnsupportedVersion(u8),
    
    /// Protocol negotiation failed
    ProtocolMismatch,
    
    /// Server rejected upgrade
    Rejected(StatusCode),
}
```

## Sec-WebSocket-Accept Calculation

The accept key is computed as:
```rust
fn compute_accept_key(client_key: &str) -> String {
    use sha1::{Sha1, Digest};
    use base64::Engine;
    
    const GUID: &str = "258EAFA5-E914-47DA-95CA-C5AB0DC85B11";
    
    let mut hasher = Sha1::new();
    hasher.update(client_key.as_bytes());
    hasher.update(GUID.as_bytes());
    let hash = hasher.finalize();
    
    base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(hash)
}
```

## URL Parsing

```rust
pub fn parse_ws_url(url: &str) -> Result<(String, u16, String, bool), UrlError> {
    // ws://host:port/path -> (host, port, path, false)
    // wss://host:port/path -> (host, port, path, true)
    // Default ports: ws=80, wss=443
}
```

## Client Flow

```rust
pub async fn client_handshake(
    cx: &Cx,
    stream: TcpStream,
    handshake: ClientHandshake,
) -> Outcome<WebSocket, HandshakeError> {
    // 1. Send HTTP upgrade request
    let request = handshake.request();
    stream.write_all(cx, &request.to_bytes()).await?;
    
    // 2. Read HTTP response
    let response = read_http_response(cx, &mut stream).await?;
    
    // 3. Validate response
    handshake.validate_response(&response)?;
    
    // 4. Return WebSocket on success
    Ok(WebSocket::from_upgraded(stream, Role::Client))
}
```

## Server Flow

```rust
pub async fn server_handshake(
    cx: &Cx,
    stream: TcpStream,
    config: ServerHandshake,
) -> Outcome<(WebSocket, Request<()>), HandshakeError> {
    // 1. Read HTTP request
    let request = read_http_request(cx, &mut stream).await?;
    
    // 2. Validate and generate response
    let response = config.accept(&request)?;
    
    // 3. Send response
    stream.write_all(cx, &response.to_bytes()).await?;
    
    // 4. Return WebSocket and original request
    Ok((WebSocket::from_upgraded(stream, Role::Server), request))
}
```

## TLS Integration

For wss:// URLs:
```rust
pub async fn connect_wss(
    cx: &Cx,
    url: &str,
    tls_config: TlsConfig,
) -> Outcome<WebSocket, WsError> {
    let (host, port, path, _) = parse_ws_url(url)?;
    
    // 1. TCP connect
    let tcp = TcpStream::connect(cx, (host.as_str(), port)).await?;
    
    // 2. TLS handshake
    let tls = TlsConnector::new(tls_config)
        .connect(cx, &host, tcp)
        .await?;
    
    // 3. WebSocket handshake
    client_handshake(cx, tls, handshake).await
}
```

## Testing

### Unit Tests
- Accept key computation
- URL parsing
- Request/response generation
- Validation logic

### Integration Tests
- Successful handshake
- Rejected handshake
- Invalid key
- Subprotocol negotiation
- Extension negotiation (basic)

### Interop Tests
- Connect to echo.websocket.org
- Connect to local test server

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Client handshake works
- [ ] Server handshake works
- [ ] TLS (wss://) supported
- [ ] Subprotocol negotiation
- [ ] Extension negotiation (basic)
- [ ] Error handling complete
- [ ] Interop with standard servers

+{"id":"bd-1kg0","title":"WebSocket handshake: HTTP upgrade negotiation","description":"# WebSocket Handshake Implementation

## Goal

Implement the WebSocket opening handshake (HTTP upgrade) for both client and server roles.

## Background

WebSocket connections start as HTTP/1.1 and upgrade via the Upgrade header mechanism.

### Client Request
```http
GET /chat HTTP/1.1
Host: server.example.com
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Key: dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBub25jZQ==
Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13
Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: chat, superchat
Sec-WebSocket-Extensions: permessage-deflate
```

### Server Response
```http
HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Accept: s3pPLMBiTxaQ9kYGzzhZRbK+xOo=
Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: chat
```

## Types to Implement

### ClientHandshake
```rust
pub struct ClientHandshake {
    /// WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://)
    url: Url,
    
    /// Random 16-byte key, base64 encoded
    key: String,
    
    /// Requested subprotocols (optional)
    protocols: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Requested extensions (optional)
    extensions: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Additional headers
    headers: HeaderMap,
}

impl ClientHandshake {
    /// Generate the HTTP upgrade request
    pub fn request(&self) -> Request<()>;
    
    /// Validate server response
    pub fn validate_response(&self, response: &Response<()>) -> Result<(), HandshakeError>;
}
```

### ServerHandshake
```rust
pub struct ServerHandshake {
    /// Supported subprotocols
    supported_protocols: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Supported extensions
    supported_extensions: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Custom validation callback
    validator: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&Request<()>) -> bool>>,
}

impl ServerHandshake {
    /// Validate client request and generate response
    pub fn accept(&self, request: &Request<()>) -> Result<Response<()>, HandshakeError>;
    
    /// Reject with custom status code
    pub fn reject(status: StatusCode) -> Response<()>;
}
```

### HandshakeError
```rust
pub enum HandshakeError {
    /// Invalid HTTP request
    InvalidRequest(String),
    
    /// Missing required header
    MissingHeader(&'static str),
    
    /// Invalid Sec-WebSocket-Key
    InvalidKey,
    
    /// Invalid Sec-WebSocket-Accept (response validation)
    InvalidAccept,
    
    /// Unsupported WebSocket version
    UnsupportedVersion(u8),
    
    /// Protocol negotiation failed
    ProtocolMismatch,
    
    /// Server rejected upgrade
    Rejected(StatusCode),
}
```

## Sec-WebSocket-Accept Calculation

The accept key is computed as:
```rust
fn compute_accept_key(client_key: &str) -> String {
    use sha1::{Sha1, Digest};
    use base64::Engine;
    
    const GUID: &str = "258EAFA5-E914-47DA-95CA-C5AB0DC85B11";
    
    let mut hasher = Sha1::new();
    hasher.update(client_key.as_bytes());
    hasher.update(GUID.as_bytes());
    let hash = hasher.finalize();
    
    base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(hash)
}
```

## URL Parsing

```rust
pub fn parse_ws_url(url: &str) -> Result<(String, u16, String, bool), UrlError> {
    // ws://host:port/path -> (host, port, path, false)
    // wss://host:port/path -> (host, port, path, true)
    // Default ports: ws=80, wss=443
}
```

## Client Flow

```rust
pub async fn client_handshake(
    cx: &Cx,
    stream: TcpStream,
    handshake: ClientHandshake,
) -> Outcome<WebSocket, HandshakeError> {
    // 1. Send HTTP upgrade request
    let request = handshake.request();
    stream.write_all(cx, &request.to_bytes()).await?;
    
    // 2. Read HTTP response
    let response = read_http_response(cx, &mut stream).await?;
    
    // 3. Validate response
    handshake.validate_response(&response)?;
    
    // 4. Return WebSocket on success
    Ok(WebSocket::from_upgraded(stream, Role::Client))
}
```

## Server Flow

```rust
pub async fn server_handshake(
    cx: &Cx,
    stream: TcpStream,
    config: ServerHandshake,
) -> Outcome<(WebSocket, Request<()>), HandshakeError> {
    // 1. Read HTTP request
    let request = read_http_request(cx, &mut stream).await?;
    
    // 2. Validate and generate response
    let response = config.accept(&request)?;
    
    // 3. Send response
    stream.write_all(cx, &response.to_bytes()).await?;
    
    // 4. Return WebSocket and original request
    Ok((WebSocket::from_upgraded(stream, Role::Server), request))
}
```

## TLS Integration

For wss:// URLs:
```rust
pub async fn connect_wss(
    cx: &Cx,
    url: &str,
    tls_config: TlsConfig,
) -> Outcome<WebSocket, WsError> {
    let (host, port, path, _) = parse_ws_url(url)?;
    
    // 1. TCP connect
    let tcp = TcpStream::connect(cx, (host.as_str(), port)).await?;
    
    // 2. TLS handshake
    let tls = TlsConnector::new(tls_config)
        .connect(cx, &host, tcp)
        .await?;
    
    // 3. WebSocket handshake
    client_handshake(cx, tls, handshake).await
}
```

## Testing

- Accept key computation
- URL parsing
- Request/response generation
- Validation logic

### Integration Tests
- Successful handshake
- Rejected handshake
- Invalid key
- Subprotocol negotiation
- Extension negotiation (basic)

### Interop Tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Client handshake works
- [ ] Server handshake works
- [ ] TLS (wss://) supported

- [ ] Extension negotiation (basic)
- [ ] Error handling complete
- [ ] Interop with standard servers

 {"id":"bd-1ndg","title":"Async I/O Traits Verification Suite (unit tests, adapters)","description":"# Async I/O Traits Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for async I/O traits (imz, tokio-io equivalent) ensuring correct trait implementations and adapter behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- AsyncRead: poll_read, read_buf
- AsyncWrite: poll_write, poll_flush, poll_shutdown
- AsyncBufRead: poll_fill_buf, consume
- AsyncSeek: poll_complete, start_seek
- Utility: read_to_end, read_to_string, read_exact
- Utility: write_all, copy, duplex

### 2. Adapter Tests
- BufReader: buffered reading
- BufWriter: buffered writing
- BufStream: bidirectional buffering
- Chain: sequential reading
- Take: limited reading
- Split: byte delimiter splitting
- Lines: line iterator
- ReadHalf/WriteHalf: split I/O

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Copy stream | Efficient I/O copy |
| Buffered read chain | Adapter composition |
| Cancel during read_to_end | Partial result safety |
| Duplex echo | Bidirectional I/O |
| Split + join | Reader/writer split |

### 4. Trait Compliance Tests
- Implementors: TcpStream, File, etc.
- Contract verification: flush on drop
- Cancel-safety for all operations

## Logging Requirements
- I/O events logged with buffer sizes
- On failure: dump adapter state
- Byte throughput metrics

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All traits have unit tests
- [ ] All adapters have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass
- [ ] Trait compliance verified
- [ ] `cargo test io` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib io
cargo test --test io_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"FrostyCanyon","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:49:07.382659203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:13:23.906638504Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:13:23.906561130Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. 835 IO unit tests (AsyncRead, AsyncWrite, AsyncBufRead, adapters: BufReader, BufWriter, Chain, Take, Split), 17 IO E2E tests (read/write, split, cancel, region close, replay determinism), 21 IO cancellation tests (feature-gated).  {"id":"bd-yt6g","title":"Epic: DPOR & Formal Verification Tooling (Phase 5)","description":"# Epic: DPOR & Formal Verification Tooling (Phase 5)

## Overview

This epic implements Dynamic Partial Order Reduction (DPOR) for systematic schedule exploration, plus integration with formal verification tools like TLA+. Together, these provide high-assurance testing of concurrent code.

## Background & Context

The Asupersync roadmap defines Phase 5 as 'DPOR + TLA+ tooling'. This is the capstone of the testing story:

- **Phase 0-1**: Lab runtime with deterministic scheduling
- **Phase 2-4**: Oracles, chaos testing, distributed simulation
- **Phase 5**: Systematic exploration to find ALL bugs

## The Problem

Random testing (even with many seeds) can miss bugs:
- Some interleavings are rare
- Bug-triggering schedules may never be sampled
- No coverage guarantees

DPOR solves this: systematically explore distinct schedules, pruning equivalent ones.

## What is DPOR?

Dynamic Partial Order Reduction:
1. **Run program** with one schedule
2. **Identify races**: places where different orders give different results
3. **Backtrack**: try alternative orders at race points
4. **Prune**: skip schedules equivalent to already-explored ones

Result: explore O(races) schedules instead of O(n!) interleavings.

## Key Concepts

### Happens-Before Relation
Events a and b are **independent** if:
- Different threads/tasks
- Don't access same memory
- Commuting operations

Independent events can be reordered without changing outcome.

### Mazurkiewicz Traces
Equivalence classes of executions under independent event reordering.
DPOR explores one representative per equivalence class.

### Backtracking
When a race is found:
1. Save state at the decision point
2. Continue current exploration
3. Later, restore state and try alternative

## Architecture

```
ScheduleExplorer
├── ExecutionTree         # Tree of explored schedules
├── RaceDetector          # Identifies happens-before violations
├── BacktrackSet          # Pending alternative schedules
├── StateCheckpoint       # Snapshot for backtracking
├── CoverageTracker       # What's been explored
└── ReportGenerator       # Bug reports with replay traces
```

## Relationship to Existing Code

The codebase already has scaffolding:
- src/lab/explorer.rs: ScheduleExplorer, CoverageMetrics
- src/lab/oracle.rs: Invariant verification
- src/trace/: Trace capture and replay

Phase 5 completes these into a working DPOR implementation.

## TLA+ Integration

TLA+ is a formal specification language. Integration options:
1. **Export traces**: Convert Asupersync traces to TLA+ format
2. **Check traces**: Verify traces satisfy TLA+ spec
3. **Import specs**: Generate test harnesses from TLA+ specs

## Success Criteria

- DPOR explores schedules systematically
- Provably covers all distinct interleavings (up to equivalence)
- Finds bugs that random testing misses
- Reasonable performance (< 1min for small programs)
- TLA+ traces can be verified against specs
- Clear bug reports with replay instructions

## Dependencies

This epic BLOCKS on:
- bd-1j64: Runtime Core Maturity (stable lab runtime)
- bd-z6qn: Lab/production determinism parity

## References

- asupersync_plan_v4.md Section 7.3: Schedule Exploration
- 'Partial-Order Methods for the Verification of Concurrent Systems' (Godefroid)
- 'Dynamic Partial-Order Reduction for Model Checking Software' (Flanagan & Godefroid)
- Loom library for Rust (inspiration, not dependency)

## Non-Goals

- Real-time model checking (too slow)
- Full TLA+ model checker (use existing TLC)
- Verification of arbitrary Rust code (focus on Asupersync programs)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:25:51.260875521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:25.698760060Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dpor","formal","phase5","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yt6g","depends_on_id":"bd-1j64","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:23.483738060Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yt6g","depends_on_id":"bd-z6qn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:25.698730204Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-zd26","title":"Task inspector: detailed task state, await points, and waker info","description":"# Task Inspector

## Overview
Deep-dive diagnostic view for individual tasks, showing detailed state, await points,
waker information, and execution history.

## Design

### Task Details View
```rust
pub struct TaskInspector {
    state: Arc<RuntimeState>,
    console: Console,
}

impl TaskInspector {
    /// Inspect a specific task.
    pub fn inspect(&self, task_id: TaskId) -> TaskDetails {
        let task = self.state.get_task(task_id);
        
        TaskDetails {
            id: task_id,
            state: task.state(),
            region: task.region_id(),
            spawn_location: task.spawn_location(),
            last_poll_time: task.last_poll_time(),
            poll_count: task.poll_count(),
            await_point: task.current_await_point(),
            waker_info: task.waker_info(),
            budget_consumed: task.budget_consumed(),
        }
    }
    
    /// List all tasks with summary info.
    pub fn list_tasks(&self) -> Vec<TaskSummary> {
        self.state.all_tasks()
            .map(|t| TaskSummary {
                id: t.id(),
                name: t.name(),
                state: t.state(),
                region: t.region_id(),
            })
            .collect()
    }
}
```

### Output Format
```
Task Inspector: TaskId(42)
╭────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ State:         Polling                         │
│ Region:        Region-2 (HttpHandler)          │
│ Spawned at:    src/http/handler.rs:142         │
│ Last poll:     2.3ms ago                       │
│ Poll count:    17                              │
│ Budget used:   1,234 ops                       │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Current Await Point:                           │
│   -> TcpStream::read() at src/net/tcp.rs:89    │
│   -> IoDriver::poll_ready()                    │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Waker Info:                                    │
│   Type: WorkStealingWaker                      │
│   Registered: yes                              │
│   Wake count: 3                                │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Interactive Features (TUI)
- Navigate task list with keyboard
- Click to inspect task
- Filter by state (pending, polling, completed)
- Sort by poll count, age, budget

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Task list view with sorting/filtering
- [ ] Detailed task inspection
- [ ] Await point identification
- [ ] Waker state display
- [ ] Budget tracking per task
- [ ] TUI navigation
- [ ] Unit tests

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `inspector::tests::list_all_tasks` - List tasks correctly
- `inspector::tests::inspect_running_task` - Running task details
- `inspector::tests::inspect_pending_task` - Pending task details
- `inspector::tests::await_point_detection` - Current await point
- `inspector::tests::waker_info_display` - Waker state

### Integration Tests
- `inspector::integration::live_runtime` - Inspect real runtime
- `inspector::integration::concurrent_updates` - Handle state changes

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual field lookups
- DEBUG: Task inspection events
- INFO: Task list queries","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:36.393748170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:07.009882777Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zd26","depends_on_id":"bd-nugw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:07.009850087Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-zd26","depends_on_id":"bd-vpkn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:50.500475009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

            region: task.region_id(),
            spawn_location: task.spawn_location(),
            last_poll_time: task.last_poll_time(),
            poll_count: task.poll_count(),
            await_point: task.current_await_point(),
            waker_info: task.waker_info(),
            budget_consumed: task.budget_consumed(),
        }
    }
    
    /// List all tasks with summary info.
    pub fn list_tasks(&self) -> Vec<TaskSummary> {
        self.state.all_tasks()
            .map(|t| TaskSummary {
                id: t.id(),
                name: t.name(),
                state: t.state(),
                region: t.region_id(),
            })
            .collect()
    }
}
```

## Goal

Create comprehensive end-to-end tests for all combinators (join\!, race\!, select\!, timeout\!, etc.) with emphasis on cancel-correctness and obligation safety.

## Test Directory Structure

├── combinator/
│   ├── mod.rs

│   │   ├── select_patterns.rs    # Common select\! patterns
│   │   ├── timeout_behavior.rs   # Timeout scenarios
│   │   ├── nested.rs             # Nested combinators
│   │   └── obligations.rs        # Obligation tracking across branches
│   ├── unit/
│   │   ├── join_n.rs             # Join2..Join16 tests
│   │   ├── race_n.rs             # Race2..Race16 tests
│   │   ├── outcome_aggregation.rs # Severity lattice
│   │   └── macro_expansion.rs    # Verify macro output
│   └── cancel_correctness/
│       ├── loser_drain.rs        # Losers fully drained
│       ├── obligation_cleanup.rs # No leaks on cancel
│       └── budget_respect.rs     # Cleanup within budget
```

## Critical Cancel-Correctness Tests

### Loser Drain Verification
This is THE critical invariant: race losers MUST be fully drained, not just dropped.

```rust
/// Test: race\! loser completes full cancellation protocol
#[test]
fn test_race_loser_fully_drained() {
    let lab = LabRuntime::new(LabConfig::default().seed(42));
    
    let drain_complete = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
    let drain_complete_clone = drain_complete.clone();
    
    lab.run(|cx| async move {
        let winner = race\!(
            async { 
                // Fast winner
                42 
            },
            async {
                // Slow loser with cleanup
                cx.sleep(Duration::from_secs(100)).await;
                // This should run during cancellation drain
                drain_complete_clone.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
                0
            }
        ).await;
        
        // Winner returns immediately
        assert_eq\!(winner.first().unwrap(), 42);
    });
    
    // CRITICAL: Loser's drain phase must have completed
    assert\!(drain_complete.load(Ordering::SeqCst), 
        'Loser was dropped without drain\!');
    
    // Verify via oracle
    assert\!(lab.loser_drain_oracle().is_ok());
}

/// Test: race\! with obligations in loser branch
#[test]
fn test_race_loser_obligations_resolved() {
    let lab = LabRuntime::new(config);
    
    lab.run(|cx| async move {
        let (tx, rx) = channel(10);
        
        race\!(
            async {
                // Winner: immediate return
                'winner'
            },
            async {
                // Loser: holds obligation
                let permit = tx.reserve(cx).await?;
                cx.sleep(Duration::from_secs(100)).await;
                permit.send('message');  // Never reached
                'loser'
            }
        ).await;
        
        // Loser's permit must be aborted, not leaked
    });
    
    assert\!(lab.obligation_leak_oracle().is_ok());
}
```

        async {
            let permit = tx.reserve().await?;
            permit.send(1);
            Ok(())
        },
        async {
            let permit = tx.reserve().await?;
            permit.send(2);
            Ok(())
        }
    )
}

## Test Script

`scripts/test_combinators.sh`:
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_DIR='./test_logs/combinators'
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

echo '=== Combinator Unit Tests ==='
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --test combinator_unit -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/unit.log"

echo '=== Cancel-Correctness Tests (CRITICAL) ==='
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --test combinator_cancel -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/cancel.log"

echo '=== Combinator E2E Tests ==='
RUST_LOG=info cargo test --test combinator_e2e -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/e2e.log"

echo '=== Obligation Safety Tests ==='
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --test combinator_obligations -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/obligations.log"

# Extract oracle results
grep -E '(loser_drain_oracle|obligation_leak_oracle)' "$LOG_DIR/"*.log

echo 'All combinator tests passed\!'
```
